channel.title: Whalesbook Financial News
channel.description: Latest Indian and global financial market news

item.title: Muthoot Microfin Posts Profit Turnaround, Sector Risks Linger
item.description: Muthoot Microfin swung to a ₹71.1 crore net profit in the March quarter, reversing a ₹401.1 crore loss last year. Strong loan growth, improved asset quality, and surging disbursements fueled the turnaround, with Assets Under Management surpassing ₹14,000 crore. Despite Gross NPAs falling to 3.89%, a negative trailing P/E ratio indicates the recovery is early, facing sector-wide caution on responsible lending.

item.title: India, ASEAN Revamp Trade Pact, Connect UPI to Vietnam System
item.description: India and ASEAN are set to update their trade agreement by year-end. Prime Minister Modi announced plans to link India's UPI with Vietnam's payment system, aiming to boost bilateral trade and investment. This move targets $25 billion in trade with Vietnam by 2030 and enhances supply chain resilience across critical minerals, agri-exports, and pharmaceuticals.

item.title: South Indian Bank Posts Record Profit Amid NIM Pressure and Risks
item.description: South Indian Bank reported a record net profit of ₹1,455 crore for fiscal year 2026, an 11.69% increase driven by lower provisions and a focus on retail and MSME lending. Gross advances rose 14.50% to ₹1,00,274 crore, with gross NPAs improving significantly to 1.43%. However, net interest margins (NIMs) fell annually to 2.95%, despite sequential gains. The bank proposed a dividend of ₹0.45 per share.

item.title: Mahindra Lifespaces Launches ₹1,650 Cr Ultra-Luxury Mumbai Project
item.description: Mahindra Lifespace Developers Ltd. has launched Mahindra BeaconHill, an ultra-premium residential development in Mahalaxmi, South Mumbai, with a projected gross development value (GDV) of ₹1,650 crore. The 58-storey tower will offer 198 residences, marking the company's strategic return to the prime South Mumbai market. This launch follows robust FY26 sales growth and a cash-surplus balance sheet for the company. The stock saw a modest gain on the announcement.

item.title: AI Agent Creates Corporation; Solana Speeds Up, Ripple Shares Hacker Data
item.description: An AI agent named Manfred has autonomously established its own legal corporation, obtaining an Employer Identification Number (EIN) and bank account. In parallel, the Solana blockchain is preparing for its Alpenglow upgrade to enhance transaction speed and predictability. Ripple is now sharing intelligence on North Korean hacking methods with the crypto industry, while Cloudflare warns of AI agents fundamentally altering the internet's advertising-driven business model.

item.title: Paytm Hits Annual Profit Milestone, But Q4 Dip Fuels Scrutiny
item.description: One97 Communications (Paytm) reported its first annual net profit of ₹552 crore, with Q4 FY26 net profit at ₹183 crore. Despite rising transaction volumes and financial services growth, profit sequentially declined from the prior quarter, raising margin concerns. The company faces intense competition, significant regulatory scrutiny, and an evolving fintech sector prioritizing sustainable, compliant operations.

item.title: India's Healthcare Shift: Phydigital Patient Programs Drive Growth
item.description: India's healthcare sector is undergoing a significant transition, moving value creation from hospitals to continuous patient engagement. The rise of 'phydigital' healthcare, blending physical care with digital tools, is transforming patient support programs into a scalable business model. This approach addresses critical challenges in managing chronic diseases, improves treatment adherence, and expands access to healthcare services beyond major cities.

item.title: Uber Tops Q2 Bookings Outlook on Strong US Demand, Shares Climb
item.description: Uber Technologies Inc. forecast robust Q2 gross bookings of $56.25 to $57.75 billion, exceeding analyst expectations. Strong U.S. rideshare demand and moderating insurance costs are driving trip growth. The company also reaffirmed its 2028 target for its enterprise segment to reach $10 billion in bookings, noting minimal impact from global tensions.

item.title: Mindspace REIT Locks In 7.63% AAA Debt For 10 Years
item.description: Mindspace Business Parks REIT has secured ₹500 crore through a 10-year, 7.63% coupon NCD issuance, fully subscribed by a major life insurer and rated AAA by Crisil and ICRA. This move strategically refinances existing debt, aiming to lock in fixed borrowing costs, mitigate interest rate volatility, and bolster cash flow stability. The REIT's cumulative capital raised now stands at approximately ₹16,400 crore, reflecting sustained investor confidence in its financial management.

item.title: India Inc. Leverages Risk Transfer to Drive Growth Amid Volatility
item.description: Indian companies are changing how they handle geopolitical risks, turning them from problems into growth opportunities. A surge of over 30% in political risk insurance demand shows a shift towards proactive protection and growth. As global trade and policy become more volatile, Indian firms are using geopolitical insights in their core strategies to navigate disruptions, secure supply chains, and build resilience.

item.title: Paytm Profit Falls 18% as Costs Squeeze Margins
item.description: One 97 Communications (Paytm) reported an 18.2% quarter-over-quarter drop in Q4 FY2026 net profit to ₹184 crore, despite revenue rising 3.2% to ₹2,264 crore. Earnings (EBITDA) also fell 5.4% to ₹132 crore, shrinking operating margins to 5.8% from 7.1% previously. This signals ongoing struggles to turn revenue growth into profit due to rising costs and competition.

item.title: Meesho's AI Pivot Fuels Discovery, Drives Record Growth
item.description: Meesho is fully embracing an AI-first strategy, shifting its e-commerce model to prioritize AI-led discovery instead of traditional search. With over 70% of its code AI-generated, more than 75% of orders now come from personalized feeds powered by its PRISM engine. This AI focus drove strong financial results, including a 43% rise in Net Merchandise Value (NMV) and an 88% reduction in losses for Q4 FY26. The company is also building India-specific AI tools like Vaani and GeoIndia LLM to serve local needs.

item.title: Hut 8 Taps $9.8 Billion AI Deal to Pivot From Crypto Mining
item.description: Hut 8 Entertainment Enterprises Inc. has secured a $9.8 billion, 15-year lease for an AI data center in Texas, marking a strategic pivot away from struggling Bitcoin mining operations. Despite current losses in crypto mining, the company is leveraging its infrastructure to capture demand in the booming AI market, adding 352 MW of contracted capacity for a total of 597 MW in AI deals. The move comes as industry-wide pressures force many miners toward higher-margin AI services.

item.title: Zee Sues Reliance-Disney JV JioStar for $3M Over Music Rights
item.description: Zee Entertainment is suing the Reliance-Disney joint venture, JioStar, for $3 million. Zee alleges JioStar used its music without permission after licensing deals expired. The lawsuit highlights the growing disputes over content rights in India's changing media and entertainment sector. A New Delhi court has ordered JioStar to stop infringing on Zee's works.

item.title: Sebi Mandates New NSE Directors to Strengthen Governance for IPO
item.description: The Securities and Exchange Board of India (Sebi) has approved the appointment of Viral Mody and Sanjay Shorey as Executive Directors (EDs) at the National Stock Exchange (NSE). These appointments meet a Sebi requirement to add dedicated leaders for critical operations and compliance. Mody, with two decades at NSE, will lead operations, while Shorey will head regulatory functions. These moves precede NSE's planned IPO, signaling a push for strong governance.

item.title: Indian Markets Surge on US-Iran De-escalation Hopes; Crude Oil Drops
item.description: Indian markets surged, led by a drop in Brent crude on US-Iran de-escalation hopes. The BSE Sensex and Nifty posted significant gains, with total market capitalization reaching ₹473 trillion. The Union Cabinet's approval of a new ₹18,100 crore emergency credit guarantee program also boosted sentiment. However, investors remain cautious amid lingering inflation risks, tighter bank liquidity, and high stock valuations.

item.title: NYSE Partners Warn: Synthetic Tokens Risk Misleading Retail Investors
item.description: Intercontinental Exchange (ICE) and its partners are flagging serious risks from synthetic tokenized stocks. Executives from NYSE, OKX, and Securitize warned that offshore products often mislead retail investors by not representing real equities, especially during corporate events. NYSE plans a regulated platform for tokenized U.S. equities, focusing on compliance first.

item.title: Meesho Reduces In-House Logistics Use to Cut Costs, Boost Profit
item.description: Meesho's in-house logistics unit, Valmo, now handles about 50% of shipments, down from its peak. This change prioritizes cost savings and efficiency over full internalisation, aligning with hybrid logistics trends and Valmo's cost advantage for affordable products.

item.title: Supreme Court Approves Alpha Corp Plan for 3 Stalled Projects, Buyers Get Relief
item.description: The Supreme Court has approved Alpha Corp Development's plan to take over three stalled projects from Earth Infrastructures Limited. The move will bring relief to over 3,000 homebuyers in Greater Noida and Gurgaon. Alpha Corp will invest Rs 750 crore to finish the projects, aiming for Rs 1,200 crore in revenue, clearing dues over 24 months without burdening buyers.

item.title: India's Rs 11.2 Trillion Power Plan Faces Smart Meter Data Hurdles
item.description: India's power distribution sector plans a massive Rs 11.2 trillion investment by 2035, aiming to install 35 crore smart meters. However, success faces major hurdles: utilities use data poorly, consumers resist adoption, infrastructure integration is tough, and Aggregate Technical & Commercial (AT&C) losses remain high in many states. Power Grid Corporation of India, a key player, trades at a P/E of about 19 and its stock has seen moderate gains year-to-date amid a complex sector outlook.

item.title: Meenakshi India Launches D2C Brand SHORTSTOP for Online Market
item.description: Meenakshi India Ltd (MIL), a long-time apparel manufacturer, is launching into the direct-to-consumer (D2C) market with its new brand, SHORTSTOP. The company has also unveiled its online store, shortstop.in, to connect directly with customers and tap into India's growing online apparel sector. SHORTSTOP offers men's shorts made from recycled, eco-friendly fibers with special comfort techniques. MIL plans to significantly expand the brand's product range by 2027.

item.title: Bajaj Auto Announces ₹5,633 Cr Share Buyback Boosted by Q4 Earnings
item.description: Bajaj Auto announced a ₹5,633 crore share buyback program, priced at ₹12,000 per share, a 16.3% premium. The move follows a strong fourth quarter for fiscal year 2026, with net profits surging 34% to ₹2,746 crore on 31.8% revenue growth. The company also declared a ₹150 per share dividend.

item.title: Home First Finance Reports 43% Q4 Profit Jump, Capital Raise Spurs Growth
item.description: Home First Finance Company India Ltd. (HFFC) reported a 42.8% year-on-year increase in consolidated net profit to ₹149.4 crore for the fourth quarter ended March 2026. Net interest income grew 36.5% to ₹306.1 crore. Assets under management rose 24.9% to ₹15,878 crore, supported by record loan disbursements of ₹1,572 crore. The company also raised ₹1,250 crore in equity, enhancing its capital adequacy.

item.title: Telangana Plans ₹38,595 Cr Hyderabad Metro Expansion After Takeover
item.description: Telangana plans a major ₹38,595 crore expansion for Hyderabad Metro, adding 122.9 km across seven corridors. This Phase II project follows the state's recent takeover of Phase I operations from L&T, aiming to boost city mobility as rapid growth continues. The state is also pursuing federal approval and planning a new Phase III corridor to the airport.

item.title: Aditya Birla Capital Profit Up, But Margins Dip as Unsecured Loans Grow
item.description: Aditya Birla Capital reported a 19% year-on-year rise in Q4 net profit to ₹780 crore. Asset growth climbed 27%, driven by personal and unsecured loans. However, net interest margins saw a slight dip from competition and market losses. Credit costs fell to a five-year low. The company's insurance business achieved strong growth in new business value and premium income, with plans for further margin and asset growth.

item.title: Bajaj Auto Posts Record Quarter: Profit Jumps 34%, ₹5,633 Cr Buyback Approved
item.description: Bajaj Auto posted a record-breaking Q4 FY26 with a 34% YoY net profit jump to ₹2,746 crore on 32% higher revenue of ₹16,006 crore. Driven by strong domestic and export volume growth, improved realizations, and a favorable product mix, the company also announced a ₹5,633 crore share buyback and a ₹150 per share dividend. Operating margins expanded to 20.8%.

item.title: Nestlé India Absorbs Costs for Volume, Ignoring Peers' Price Hikes
item.description: Nestlé India is avoiding immediate price hikes to manage input cost pressures, aiming for efficiency and volume-led growth instead of following competitors like HUL and Dabur. The company posted strong Q1 FY26 results, with revenue rising 22.6% and net profit up 27.19%. However, this strategy, alongside a high P/E ratio, raises concerns about margin sustainability and valuation if inflation continues. Analysts are mostly positive, citing brand strength and operational skill, but a forecast for lower monsoon rainfall adds uncertainty for rural demand.

item.title: India and Vietnam Set $25B Trade Goal by 2030, Prioritizing Tech and Minerals
item.description: India and Vietnam have inked an enhanced comprehensive strategic partnership, targeting $25 billion in bilateral trade by 2030, up from $16 billion last fiscal. Thirteen MoUs cover digital payments, rare earths, and emerging technologies. The pact aims to boost supply chain resilience and diversify economic ties amid changing global dynamics, supporting both nations' development goals.

item.title: Bajaj Auto Reports Record Quarter, Unveils Large Buyback and Dividend
item.description: Bajaj Auto posted a record Q4 FY26 with revenue up 32% to ₹16,006 crore and profit more than doubling to ₹3,661.9 crore. This profit boost included a one-time gain from consolidating its KTM investment. The company announced a ₹5,633 crore share buyback at ₹12,000 per share and a ₹150 per share dividend, underscoring strong returns amidst robust growth across its domestic, EV, and export segments in a competitive auto market.

item.title: Bajaj Auto approves ₹150 dividend, ₹5,633 crore buyback on record Q4 profit
item.description: Bajaj Auto Ltd. posted a substantial 34% year-on-year increase in net profit for Q4 FY26, reaching ₹2,746 crore, while revenue grew 31.8% to ₹16,005.65 crore. Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortisation (EBITDA) rose 36%. The company announced a ₹150 per share dividend and a ₹5,633 crore share buyback, its largest in four years. The stock climbed nearly 3% following the announcements.

item.title: Brigade Enterprises Issues Bonus Despite 41% Profit Drop
item.description: Brigade Enterprises approved a 1:3 bonus share issue, its first since 2019, despite a significant 41% year-on-year decline in fourth-quarter net profit. The company's consolidated revenue also saw a slight dip, with operating margins contracting. This move comes as the Indian real estate sector shows robust growth, though Brigade's stock has underperformed its peers and the broader market over the past year.

item.title: Bajaj Auto: Q4 Profit Surges 34%, ₹9,825 Cr Payout Announced
item.description: Bajaj Auto reported a strong fourth quarter for FY26. Net profit surged 34% year-on-year to ₹2,746 crore, driven by revenue growth of 31.8% to ₹16,006 crore. The company also announced a final dividend of ₹150 per share and a ₹5,633 crore share buyback, resulting in a total shareholder payout of approximately ₹9,825 crore. This significant return of capital reflects strong operational performance and aims to enhance shareholder value in a competitive market.

item.title: CESC Profit Climbs 17%, but Margins Squeeze Amid New Rules
item.description: CESC Ltd reported a 17.4% year-on-year increase in net profit for the March quarter of fiscal year 2026, reaching ₹439 crore, on revenue that rose 5.6% to ₹4,096 crore. However, operational performance faced pressure, with EBITDA down 8.5% and margins shrinking to 18.14%. The company noted a ₹35 crore impact from new labor laws and is watching regulatory changes. Despite new renewable energy deals, CESC shares closed lower on May 6 amid investor worry about shrinking margins and regulatory uncertainty.

item.title: India Exports Hit Record $863.11B in FY26, Services Surge 8.71%
item.description: India's goods and services exports reached an all-time high of $863.11 billion in fiscal year 2025-26. The robust performance, up 4.6% from the previous year, was largely propelled by an 8.71% surge in services exports. Merchandise trade saw modest growth despite global economic headwinds.

item.title: India's Energy Boom: Surging Demand Meets Climate Hurdles
item.description: India is poised to become a global energy leader, surpassing China's past influence. Fueled by rapid economic growth and demographics, the nation faces a dual task: satisfying soaring energy demand while meeting strict climate targets. This creates a high-stakes landscape for investors, requiring close watch on policy, technology, and geopolitical factors, alongside significant growth opportunities in renewables and infrastructure.

item.title: India, Vietnam Boost Ties, Sign 13 Pacts Targeting $25B Trade by 2030
item.description: India and Vietnam elevated their bilateral ties to an Enhanced Comprehensive Strategic Partnership, setting an ambitious $25 billion trade target by 2030. Thirteen agreements were signed across key sectors including digital payments, rare earths, and pharmaceuticals, signaling deeper economic and geopolitical alignment. This move aims to boost supply chain integration, fintech collaboration, and strategic cooperation in the Indo-Pacific.

item.title: Bajaj Auto Q4 Profit Doubles to ₹3,662 Cr; Approves ₹5,633 Cr Buyback
item.description: Bajaj Auto posted a strong Q4 with consolidated net profit soaring 103% to ₹3,662 crore. The company also announced a ₹5,633 crore share buyback and proposed a final dividend of ₹150 per share, signaling robust shareholder returns.

item.title: MochaTrade Targets Global Traders with US Stock Perpetual Futures
item.description: Fintech startup MochaTrade has secured undisclosed pre-seed funding from Y Combinator and Pioneer Fund. The capital will fuel the development of a platform designed to provide eligible non-US retail traders with leveraged access to US stocks, commodities, and indices via perpetual futures. This move addresses significant barriers in traditional international brokerage, aiming for continuous trading and real-time settlement.

item.title: India Growth Fund Names Naman Bagri to Drive Defence, Deep Tech Growth
item.description: India Growth Fund has appointed Naman Bagri as its new Managing Partner. The move signals a stronger focus on high-potential sectors like Defence, Deep Tech, Space, and ESG manufacturing. The fund, backed by 35North Ventures, plans to invest its ₹2,000 crore capital (with an option to raise an additional ₹500 crore) into growth-stage companies. Bagri's 20 years of experience in entrepreneurship and investment strategy will guide the fund in these key areas.

item.title: Bajaj Auto Slashes Dividend Despite Record Profits, Signaling Shift
item.description: Bajaj Auto announced a significant ₹150 per share dividend for fiscal 2025-26, alongside record Q4 financial results. However, this payout marks a notable reduction from the ₹210 per share distributed last year. Despite a 34% surge in net profit and a 32% revenue jump, the company's decision to lower the dividend payout ratio suggests a potential pivot towards reinvestment, debt reduction, or addressing emerging market dynamics. Investors are scrutinizing this move against a backdrop of positive industry growth but evolving competitive pressures.
